Varaha must have been acquainted with the works of ancient
sages on prosody such as mw, dTf% ^i^r, etc,
who have been referred to by Pihgala. Our author uses the word
in CIV. 64 like Dan^in. We cannot say definitely
whether there was a popular work of that title before our author;
or the science of metrics might have been popularly known as
Chandoviciti.
The author's knowledge of the Vedas, sacrificial lore and
propitiatory rites is stupendous. For, he summarizes, sometimes, some
Mantras and at others, quotes their initial words ; In XLIII. 55 be
quotes the initial part of the Mantra, ''didKft^TfadTTfiPS- ..." In
LXXIV.
